item.title: Bank of Maharashtra Falls 6.29%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: Bank of Maharashtra’s stock declined by 6.29% over the week ending 6 March 2026, underperforming the Sensex’s 3.00% fall. The week was marked by sharp gap down openings, heavy volume trading amid price pressure, and a sustained downtrend over three sessions, reflecting cautious investor sentiment despite the stock’s strong technical backdrop and a recent upgrade to a ‘Strong Buy’ mojo grade. Time: 18 minutes ago

item.title: Captain Polyplast Ltd Falls 6.12%: Bearish Signals and Downgrade Mark a Challenging Week
item.description: Captain Polyplast Ltd experienced a challenging week from 2 to 6 March 2026, with its stock price declining by 6.12% to close at Rs.68.01, underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00% over the same period. The week was marked by significant bearish technical signals and a downgrade to a Strong Sell rating, reflecting growing concerns about the stock’s near-term outlook despite some operational improvements. Time: 18 minutes ago

item.title: Neo Infracon Ltd Gains 1.19% Despite Downgrade: 3 Key Factors Driving the Week
item.description: Neo Infracon Ltd closed the week with a modest gain of 1.19%, ending at Rs.43.49 on 6 March 2026, despite a turbulent week marked by a significant downgrade and a valuation re-rating. The stock outperformed the Sensex, which declined 3.00% over the same period, reflecting resilience amid sector-wide pressures and mixed investor sentiment. Time: 18 minutes ago

item.title: TGB Banquets & Hotels Ltd Drops 4.31%: 3 Key Factors Behind the Weekly Decline
item.description: TGB Banquets & Hotels Ltd’s stock declined by 4.31% over the week ending 6 March 2026, closing at Rs.8.88 compared to Rs.9.28 the previous Friday. This underperformance slightly exceeded the Sensex’s 3.00% fall during the same period, reflecting persistent fundamental weaknesses and bearish technical signals amid a challenging market environment. Time: 18 minutes ago

item.title: Rallis India Ltd Falls 7.22%: Downgrade and Technical Weakness Drive the Week
item.description: Rallis India Ltd experienced a challenging week from 2 to 6 March 2026, with its stock price declining by 7.22% to close at Rs.257.60, underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00% over the same period. The week was marked by a significant gap down opening amid a recent downgrade, followed by a technical momentum shift signalling growing bearishness. Despite some intraday recoveries, the stock struggled to regain footing against broader market volatility and sector pressures. Time: 18 minutes ago

item.title: Metal Coatings (India) Ltd Falls 5.48%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Week’s Volatility
item.description: Metal Coatings (India) Ltd experienced a volatile week ending 6 March 2026, with its stock price declining 5.48% to close at Rs.54.51, underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00%. The week was marked by a sharp drop to a 52-week low, a significant intraday rebound, and a downgrade to a Strong Sell rating amid mixed valuation signals and weak fundamentals. Time: 18 minutes ago

item.title: CEAT Ltd Falls 2.07%: Technical Weakness Amid Strong Fundamentals
item.description: CEAT Ltd’s shares declined by 2.07% over the week ending 6 March 2026, closing at Rs.3,457.75, underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00%. The stock faced significant selling pressure early in the week following a downgrade to a Sell rating by MarketsMOJO, driven by a shift to mildly bearish technical momentum despite the company’s robust financial results and attractive valuation metrics. Time: 18 minutes ago

item.title: Duropack Ltd Falls 3.09%: 3 Key Factors Behind the Weekly Decline
item.description: Duropack Ltd’s shares declined by 3.09% over the week ending 6 March 2026, closing at Rs.54.00 compared to Rs.55.72 the previous Friday. This underperformance slightly exceeded the Sensex’s 3.00% fall, reflecting persistent challenges in the company’s financial health, valuation concerns, and sectoral headwinds. The week was marked by two fresh 52-week lows and a notable valuation shift signalling expensive territory despite mixed returns. Time: 19 minutes ago

item.title: Kinetic Engineering Ltd’s Mixed Week: -1.18% Price, Outperforming Sensex by 1.82%
item.description: Kinetic Engineering Ltd’s shares closed the week down 1.18% at Rs.239.20, marginally outperforming the Sensex which declined 3.00% over the same period. The stock exhibited notable volatility amid a backdrop of deteriorating fundamentals, shifting technical momentum, and a downgrade to a Strong Sell rating by MarketsMOJO. Despite short-term price fluctuations, the stock’s performance contrasted with the broader market’s sharper losses, reflecting mixed investor sentiment. Time: 19 minutes ago

item.title: Ansal Properties Gains 1.56% Despite Sector Weakness: 5 Key Factors Driving Volatility
item.description: Ansal Properties & Infrastructure Ltd closed the week at Rs.3.91, marking a modest gain of 1.56% from Rs.3.85 last Friday, while the Sensex declined 3.00% over the same period. The stock exhibited notable volatility, hitting upper circuit limits twice early in the week before succumbing to heavy selling pressure and lower circuit hits in the final sessions. This mixed performance reflects a complex interplay of strong short-term buying interest, technical upgrades, and persistent fundamental challenges amid a broadly weak market environment. Time: 19 minutes ago

item.title: CIAN Agro Industries & Infrastructure Ltd Falls 6.55%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: CIAN Agro Industries & Infrastructure Ltd experienced a challenging week from 2 to 6 March 2026, with its stock price declining by 6.55% to close at Rs.1,224.65, underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00% over the same period. The week was marked by two significant gap down openings amid sectoral and market concerns, followed by a late-week valuation shift signalling renewed price attractiveness. Despite the downward pressure, the stock’s relative volatility and technical positioning provide a nuanced picture of its near-term outlook. Time: 19 minutes ago

item.title: Kirloskar Electric Falls 8.44%: Bearish Momentum and Valuation Shift Shape Week
item.description: Kirloskar Electric Company Ltd endured a challenging week, with its share price declining 8.44% from ₹89.15 to ₹81.63, significantly underperforming the Sensex’s 3.00% fall over the same period. The stock faced sustained bearish momentum amid technical downturns and volatile trading, while a notable valuation shift enhanced its price attractiveness despite the negative price action. Time: 19 minutes ago

item.title: Hindustan Petroleum Falls 7.71%: 5 Key Factors Driving This Week's Decline
item.description: Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Ltd. (HPCL) endured a challenging week from 2 to 6 March 2026, with its stock price declining 7.71% to close at Rs.405.25, underperforming the Sensex’s 3.00% fall over the same period. The week was marked by significant volatility, technical shifts, and mixed market signals amid sector headwinds and broader market weakness. Time: 19 minutes ago

item.title: L T Foods Ltd’s Volatile Week: -2.31% Amid Sharp Swings and Institutional Interest
item.description: L T Foods Ltd closed the week ending 6 March 2026 with a modest decline of 2.31%, underperforming the broader Sensex which fell 3.00%. The stock experienced significant intraday volatility, marked by a sharp rebound midweek and strong institutional participation, contrasting with early-week price pressure and technical uncertainty. Despite the weekly loss, L T Foods demonstrated resilience relative to the market, supported by a notable surge in trading volumes and delivery activity on 5 and 6 March. Time: 19 minutes ago

item.title: Hexaware Technologies Ltd Falls 2.90% Amid 52-Week Low and Market Volatility
item.description: Hexaware Technologies Ltd’s shares declined by 2.90% over the week ending 6 March 2026, closing at Rs.459.90 compared to Rs.473.65 the previous Friday. This underperformance was slightly better than the Sensex’s 3.00% fall, reflecting persistent sector pressures and company-specific challenges including a fresh 52-week low and elevated volatility during the week. Time: 19 minutes ago

item.title: IB Infotech Enterprises Ltd Surges 21.53%: 5 Key Drivers Behind the Rally
item.description: IB Infotech Enterprises Ltd delivered a remarkable weekly performance, surging 21.53% from Rs.642.85 to Rs.781.25 between 2 and 6 March 2026, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined 3.00% over the same period. The stock hit multiple new 52-week and all-time highs, reflecting sustained momentum amid a mixed market backdrop. This review analyses the key events and price movements that shaped the week for IB Infotech. Time: 19 minutes ago

item.title: Aurionpro Solutions Ltd Declines 3.48%: 2 Key Factors Behind the Weekly Slide
item.description: Aurionpro Solutions Ltd’s stock declined by 3.48% over the week ending 6 March 2026, closing at Rs.858.85 compared to Rs.889.80 the previous Friday. This underperformance slightly exceeded the Sensex’s 3.00% fall during the same period, reflecting a challenging week marked by a fresh 52-week low and persistent technical weakness amid broader market volatility. Time: 19 minutes ago

item.title: Petronet LNG Ltd Drops 10.34%: 5 Key Factors Behind the Sharp Weekly Decline
item.description: Petronet LNG Ltd experienced a turbulent week from 2 to 6 March 2026, with its share price declining sharply by 10.34% to close at Rs.289.90, significantly underperforming the Sensex’s 3.00% fall. The stock faced intense selling pressure amid volatile trading sessions, heavy put option activity, and mixed technical signals, reflecting a cautious market stance despite a recent upgrade to a Hold rating by MarketsMOJO. Time: 20 minutes ago

item.title: EIH Ltd Gains 4.96%: 4 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Rally
item.description: EIH Ltd delivered a notable weekly gain of 4.96%, closing at Rs.329.05 on 6 March 2026, outperforming the Sensex which declined 3.00% over the same period. The stock’s performance was marked by a sharp rebound midweek following a fresh 52-week low, culminating in a robust surge that triggered an upper circuit on 5 March. This review analyses the key events shaping the stock’s volatile week and their impact on price action. Time: 20 minutes ago

item.title: Engineers India Ltd Drops 7.60%: 6 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Decline
item.description: Engineers India Ltd. experienced a challenging week from 2 to 6 March 2026, with its stock price declining 7.60% to close at Rs.204.75, underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00% over the same period. The week was marked by significant volatility, technical momentum shifts, valuation reassessments, and a downgrade in analyst rating, all contributing to the stock's subdued performance despite strong longer-term fundamentals. Time: 20 minutes ago
